why.1: The delivered module text has no commented-out old loader block; only ld/snd remain.
why.2: Bare except: still appears in both ld and snd; neither names exception types.
why.3: On load failure ld still does d = {} with no raise or ERROR log naming the file/problem.
why.4: Cryptic names remain throughout: ld, snd, p, d, r, to, pl, u, bo.
why.5: Defaults stay inline (retries 5, timeout 30, backoff 0); no module-level named constants.
why.6: No top-of-file role/design header; the '# get the retries' restating comment is still present.

why.1: Item 1 explicitly states order.status/refunded_amount are set before payments_api.refund() and a raised call leaves the order permanently marked refunded; the fix text requires committing state only after confirmed-ok.
why.2: Item 2 states a non-ok result is only logged as a warning yet treated as success (returns True); the proposed fix requires return False on non-ok and only commit fee/state after status == ok.
why.3: Item 4 flags that amount is never validated and cites no bound against negative values or the order's paid total, i.e. unvalidated input reaches the payment API.
why.4: Item 7 notes logs lack order_id/traceback, but it never quotes the bare 'refund error'/'refund not ok' strings as the problem, does not call out missing amount/payment_ref, and gives no concrete improved log message example.
why.5: Item 5 flags unguarded orders[order_id] and that it raises KeyError instead of failing gracefully.
why.6: The only concrete reordering proposed is mutate after confirmed-ok (and return False on non-ok). It does not propose the full sequence validate inputs first → call API → mutate only on success as one corrected ordering.

why.1: Both modules thread and log an explicit record_id (`path:line_num`): ingest builds it and passes it into enrich_record; enrich logs that same id on drop/success paths, so a record is correlatable across the boundary.
why.2: Both files use `logging.getLogger` with `logger.error`/`logger.warning`/`logger.debug`/`logger.info`; there are no print statements.
why.3: json.loads is wrapped in try/except JSONDecodeError; failures log at ERROR with record_id and raw line plus `exc_info=True` (parse error in the log), then `continue` so the run proceeds.
why.4: Three distinct messages: unparseable line ('failed to parse'), missing region ('missing \'region\' field; dropping'), unknown region ('has unrecognized region %r; dropping')—each names its cause.
why.5: Unknown region is logged at WARNING with record_id and the offending region_key via %r before return None; no silent drop.
why.6: Per-record success paths only log at DEBUG (enrich 'enriched', save 'saved'); run start/finish stay at INFO without flooding per-record success.

why.1: except ValueError: continue discards the error with only continue and no logging; that is exactly the silent-discard pattern the expectation forbids.
why.2: Rejected lines are not logged at all—no WARN/ERROR message, no line/sku content, no rejection reason.
why.3: try/except is inside the for-loop with continue on failure, so a bad line is skipped and remaining lines still run.
why.4: prices[sku] = float(price) runs only after split and float succeed; on ValueError nothing is written to prices.
why.5: OUTPUT never imports or calls logging; failures are handled only with continue, not a logging level.

why.1: OUTPUT has no boundary validation: it indexes payload["event_type"] and payload["invoice"] and invoice fields directly. Missing keys or a non-dict invoice raise uncaught KeyError/TypeError; the text explicitly says malformed input is left to raise naturally, not an explicit raise or ERROR/WARN + early return.
why.2: amount_cents is written with no type/sign check: ledger[invoice["id"]] = invoice["amount_cents"] with no non-negative integer (or equivalent) validation and no rejection message including invoice id and bad value.
why.3: There is no validation gate before the ledger write. A payload that is structurally present but invalid on amount (e.g. negative or non-integer amount_cents) is recorded immediately; nothing ensures mutation only after all checks pass.
why.4: No rejection path exists, so no diagnostic messages naming the failed field and received value—only uncaught exceptions or a bare return for non-invoice.paid events.
why.5: For a well-shaped invoice.paid payload, event_type is checked, then ledger[invoice id] is set to amount_cents. Other event_type values hit the early return and are not treated as errors.

why.1: find_users_by_domain uses a bare `except:` and returns []. No specific exception type is named.
why.2: On failure the function only `return []`. There is no WARN/ERROR log with domain and exception context, and the exception is not re-raised.
why.3: The new query hardcodes `limit 50` inline. No named page-size constant is introduced or used.
why.4: find_users_by_domain has no print calls; only the pre-existing add_user uses print.
why.5: The new function has no comments; the only restating comment remains the legacy `# search the users` on search_users.
why.6: SQL uses `email like ?` with the pattern bound as `("%@" + domain,)`; domain is not concatenated into the SQL string.
